definition_long: The thesis should first report cumulative target RRI and acquisition cost separately, then use scalarized objectives only when the tradeoff is explicit.
definition_short: Budget consumed to acquire observations, measured by view count, path length, elapsed time, invalid-action rate, or a weighted combination.
label: Acquisition Cost

definition_long: AUC can summarize how quickly reconstruction quality, coverage, or ranking quality improves as views are acquired or candidate thresholds change.
definition_short: Aggregate score computed by integrating a metric curve over an acquisition, threshold, or ranking axis.
label: Area Under Curve

definition_long: ADT is adjacent to ARIA-NBV's sim-to-real context; the current experiments focus on ASE and EFM3D/ATEK exports, while ADT remains a relevant transfer surface.
definition_short: Project Aria dataset with real-world captures and digital-twin scene annotations.
label: Aria Digital Twin

definition_long: AEO is relevant as a possible sim-to-real check for ARIA-NBV ideas that are first developed on ASE mesh-supervised snippets.
definition_short: Small-scale real-world Project Aria object dataset used by EFM3D for egocentric 3D perception evaluation.
label: Aria Everyday Objects

definition_long: ARIA-NBV uses ASE snippets and the public mesh-supervised subset to generate oracle RRI labels from ground-truth meshes and semi-dense point clouds.
definition_short: Large-scale synthetic indoor dataset with simulated Project Aria sensor characteristics, egocentric trajectories, and scene annotations.
label: Aria Synthetic Environments

definition_long: ARIA-NBV samples candidate views around a reference pose or target, renders candidate depths from the mesh for oracle labels, and scores candidates with RRI or learned VIN predictions.
definition_short: Proposed camera pose whose expected reconstruction utility is evaluated before selecting the next observation.
label: Candidate View

definition_long: CPF is used by Project Aria for gaze-related quantities and should remain distinct from rig, camera, world, and PyTorch3D frames in ARIA-NBV docs.
definition_short: Coordinate frame placed at the midpoint between the left and right eye boxes of Project Aria glasses.
label: Central Pupil Frame

definition_long: ARIA-NBV uses point-to-mesh and Chamfer-style distances as reconstruction error components when deriving oracle RRI labels and diagnostics.
definition_short: Bidirectional point-set distance commonly used to compare reconstructed points against reference geometry.
label: Chamfer Distance

definition_long: For the thesis-core ASE mesh/oracle loop, the transition uses candidate rendering or cached candidate observations to update the counterfactual point state and rollout history. It must be deterministic under the stored seed and lineage.
definition_short: Replayable state update after selecting a candidate view.
label: Counterfactual Transition

definition_long: Coverage ratio is a useful diagnostic baseline for NBV, but ARIA-NBV treats reconstruction-quality improvement through RRI as the preferred optimization target.
definition_short: Fraction of a target surface, scene, or region treated as observed under a chosen visibility or distance threshold.
label: Coverage Ratio

definition_long: ARIA-NBV distinguishes full 6-DoF pose estimation from reduced 5-DoF candidate or action spaces used for practical view planning.
definition_short: Number of independent pose parameters available to a camera, object, or action representation.
label: Degrees of Freedom

definition_long: The current project uses EFM3D and its EVL architecture to expose voxel occupancy, centerness, semantic, and OBB evidence for VIN-style RRI prediction.
definition_short: Egocentric 3D foundation-model stack used as the frozen spatial backbone for ARIA-NBV candidate scoring.
label: Egocentric Foundation Model 3D

definition_long: ARIA-NBV uses EVL head outputs and voxel evidence as the frozen scene representation for VIN-style RRI prediction.
definition_short: EFM3D architecture that lifts synchronized egocentric observations into a gravity-aligned 3D voxel feature volume.
label: Egocentric Voxel Lifting

definition_long: At each rollout step, ARIA-NBV samples a finite candidate set and exposes only valid candidates as admissible actions. This keeps planning bounded and preserves invalidity as a feasibility constraint.
definition_short: Masked finite action set over sampled candidate views.
label: Finite Candidate Action Set

definition_long: The mandatory M5 learned policy-like result is fitted Double-Q / Q_H over finite candidate sets. It must respect validity masks and beat one-step greedy or model scoring on cumulative target RRI under equal acquisition budget, with bounded oracle lookahead as an upper bound.
definition_short: Finite-horizon candidate-value function for target-conditioned ARIA-NBV.
label: Finite-Horizon Q Function

definition_long: The return definition keeps gamma symbolic so discounted ablations remain possible. The first thesis result should report cumulative target RRI under an equal acquisition budget and treat log-improvement or normalized rewards as follow-up analysis.
definition_short: H-step discounted return over target-RRI rewards.
label: Finite-Horizon Return

definition_long: ARIA-NBV uses 5-DoF language for candidate and planning abstractions where position and viewing direction matter while roll is not an independently optimized control dimension.
definition_short: Reduced camera-action parameterization commonly used when roll is fixed or otherwise constrained.
label: Five Degrees of Freedom

definition_long: Candidate-view frusta define which scene surfaces can project into a camera image and are therefore central to visibility, rendering, and RRI diagnostics.
definition_short: Truncated pyramidal camera-visible volume bounded by near and far clipping planes plus lateral field-of-view planes.
label: Frustum

definition_long: In ARIA-NBV, ground truth usually refers to ASE meshes, object annotations, poses, or labels used to compute oracle supervision and diagnostic metrics.
definition_short: Reference data or annotations treated as the trusted target for training, validation, or evaluation.
label: Ground Truth

definition_long: Ground-Truth Target Evaluation uses GT OBBs and target mesh crops for oracle target-RRI labels, matching checks, and evaluation while keeping those annotations hidden from the actor-visible selector, scorer, and Q_H model in the main result.
definition_short: Main thesis protocol component using ground-truth OBBs and target mesh crops only for labels and evaluation.
label: Ground-Truth Target Evaluation

definition_long: LUF is one of the coordinate-frame conventions that must stay explicit when moving between Project Aria cameras, PyTorch3D cameras, and ARIA-NBV candidate poses.
definition_short: Camera coordinate convention whose x axis points left, y axis points up, and z axis points forward.
label: Left-Up-Forward

definition_long: ARIA-NBV mainly depends on MPS-style pose and semi-dense mapping products as the current reconstruction state for RRI computation.
definition_short: Project Aria processing services that derive pose, mapping, gaze, hand, and related perception outputs from sensor recordings.
label: Machine Perception Services

definition_long: MTD supplies the logged egocentric trajectory used to define snippet state, current reconstruction context, and candidate-view reference poses.
definition_short: Device poses over time, usually represented as a sequence of 6-DoF transformations.
label: Motion Trajectory Data

definition_long: MFCD provides the egocentric image evidence consumed by EVL and aligned with pose, calibration, and semi-dense point streams.
definition_short: Synchronized camera streams from multiple Project Aria cameras over a temporal window.
label: Multi-Frame Camera Data

definition_long: MSDPD is the sparse observed geometry that ARIA-NBV fuses with candidate point clouds and compares against ground-truth meshes for RRI labels.
definition_short: Semi-dense 3D point observations generated by SLAM-style processing across a snippet or trajectory window.
label: Multi-Semi-Dense Point Data

definition_long: MVS is part of the broader reconstruction context for NBV, although ARIA-NBV's current oracle labels are built from ASE meshes and Aria-style semi-dense point observations.
definition_short: Reconstruction family that estimates dense or semi-dense scene geometry from multiple calibrated views.
label: Multi-view Stereo

definition_long: In ARIA-NBV, the preferred objective is reconstruction quality improvement rather than coverage alone, with candidate views ranked by oracle or learned RRI scores.
definition_short: Problem of selecting the next sensor viewpoint to improve an active reconstruction or inspection objective under a limited acquisition budget.
label: Next-Best-View

definition_long: Observed Target Selection uses predicted or tracked OBBs, class probabilities, confidence, projected area, and semidense or EVL support. Ground-truth target annotations are not visible to the selector in the main thesis protocol.
definition_short: Main thesis protocol component requiring target selection to use only actor-visible observed or predicted target evidence.
label: Observed Target Selection

definition_long: Occupancy-style voxel evidence appears in EVL outputs and VIN feature construction, where it helps summarize local 3D scene state for candidate scoring.
definition_short: Spatial grid whose cells encode whether space is occupied, free, unknown, or represented by a related occupancy probability.
label: Occupancy Grid

definition_long: The current oracle renders candidate depth maps from ASE ground-truth meshes, backprojects candidate point clouds, fuses them with the current semi-dense reconstruction, and scores the resulting surface-distance improvement.
definition_short: RRI label computed with privileged ground-truth geometry, used for supervised training and evaluation.
label: Oracle RRI

definition_long: OBBs are a natural target encoding for entity-aware ARIA-NBV because they provide center, extent, orientation, semantic class, and confidence signals.
definition_short: 3D bounding box with arbitrary orientation, used to represent object extent more tightly than an axis-aligned box.
label: Oriented Bounding Box

definition_long: ARIA-NBV compares current and candidate-fused point clouds against ground-truth meshes to compute oracle RRI and surface-distance diagnostics.
definition_short: Set of 3D points representing observed scene geometry.
label: Point Cloud

definition_long: Predicted-Target Q covers target-conditioned one-step scoring and finite-candidate Q_H selection whose target inputs are actor-visible predicted or observed descriptors, not ground-truth target annotations.
definition_short: Main thesis protocol component requiring scorer or Q_H inputs to use predicted or observed target descriptors.
label: Predicted-Target Q

definition_long: ARIA-NBV treats Project Aria and its MPS-style products as the actor-visible sensing contract, while ASE meshes remain offline supervision and evaluation assets.
definition_short: Egocentric research-device and tooling ecosystem for calibrated, time-aligned multimodal sensing.
label: Project Aria

definition_long: ARIA-NBV computes RRI by comparing reconstruction error before and after fusing candidate-view geometry, usually against an ASE ground-truth mesh for oracle supervision and evaluation.
definition_short: Metric quantifying the relative reconstruction-quality improvement obtained by adding a candidate observation to the current reconstruction.

formula.typst: $"RRI"(q) = (d(#(symb.oracle.points)_t, M) - d(#(symb.oracle.points)_t union #(symb.oracle.points_q), M)) / (d(#(symb.oracle.points)_t, M) + epsilon)$

label: Relative Reconstruction Improvement

definition_long: The rollout state contains accumulated semidense/current points, candidate poses, validity masks and reason codes, observed/predicted target fields, bounded history, and budget state. GT meshes, GT OBB crops, and oracle labels remain outside the actor-visible state in the main protocol.
definition_short: Actor-visible state bundle used by target-conditioned rollouts and Q_H.
label: Rollout State

definition_long: SceneScript is relevant to ARIA-NBV as a possible semantic/global planning layer and as one source of ASE scene-structure context.
definition_short: Structured language representation for indoor scene layout using primitives such as walls, doors, windows, and objects.
label: SceneScript Language

definition_long: In ARIA-NBV, logged SLAM poses and semi-dense points form the current reconstruction state against which candidate-view RRI is evaluated.
definition_short: Method for estimating sensor motion while building a map of the surrounding scene.
label: Simultaneous Localization and Mapping

definition_long: Project Aria poses, candidate cameras, and world-to-device transforms are usually represented as 6-DoF rigid-body transforms.
definition_short: Pose parameterization with three translational and three rotational degrees of freedom.
label: Six Degrees of Freedom

definition_long: A snippet typically contains RGB or grayscale streams, poses, calibration, semi-dense points, and scene metadata that EVL lifts into a voxel grid.
definition_short: Short synchronized temporal window of Aria sensor data used as one EVL/VIN input sample.
label: Snippet

definition_long: The ARIA-NBV MDP keeps actions restricted to sampled finite candidate views and keeps GT meshes or GT target crops outside the actor-visible state. It is the contract that connects target-conditioned rollout generation, reward computation, validity masks, and fitted finite-horizon Q learning.
definition_short: Finite-horizon MDP contract for target-conditioned ARIA-NBV rollouts and fitted Q_H training.
label: Target-Conditioned NBV MDP

definition_long: The scorer predicts target-specific utility so view ranking can prioritize a selected entity or region instead of only optimizing scene-level RRI.
definition_short: VIN-style candidate scorer that receives scene state, a candidate view, and an encoding of the target of interest.
label: Target-Conditioned Scorer

definition_long: Target-conditioned ARIA-NBV variants use this target as an explicit input to candidate scoring and planning instead of optimizing only scene-level RRI.
definition_short: Selected entity, object crop, point, region, or surface-deficit hypothesis whose reconstruction quality should be improved.
label: Target of Interest

definition_long: The main thesis reward is cumulative target RRI under equal acquisition budget. Log-improvement and episode-normalized variants remain visible follow-up reward ablations, not the default target for the first Q_H result.
definition_short: Quality-only immediate reward equal to target-specific RRI for the selected candidate.
label: Target-RRI Reward

definition_long: Target-specific RRI lets the thesis compare views by how much they improve a selected object or region, even when scene-level RRI would prefer large background surfaces.
definition_short: RRI computed only on the ground-truth and reconstructed geometry associated with a selected target of interest.
label: Target-Specific RRI

definition_long: ARIA-NBV treats active 3DGS work as proposal, uncertainty, and simulator-bridge literature, not as a replacement for ASE mesh-supervised RRI.
definition_short: Explicit radiance-field representation using optimized 3D Gaussian primitives for real-time novel-view synthesis.
label: 3D Gaussian Splatting

definition_long: Tracks can be triangulated or optimized into 3D points, making them a bridge between image evidence and the semi-dense point clouds used by ARIA-NBV.
definition_short: Temporal sequence of corresponding image-feature detections across frames, usually carrying per-frame image coordinates, timestamps, and camera IDs.
label: Track

definition_long: The mask m_{t,i} gates candidate actions, while invalid reason codes rho_{t,i} preserve why a candidate was rejected. Collision, outside-bounds poses, no target visibility, bad frusta, no depth hits, and outside-EVL-extent cases are constraints rather than low target-RRI examples.
definition_short: Hard mask that separates feasible candidate actions from invalid candidates.
label: Validity Mask

definition_long: ARIA-NBV adapts VIN-NBV by placing a lightweight RRI prediction head on top of frozen EVL features and candidate-pose evidence from ASE snippets.
definition_short: Learned candidate-view scorer that predicts RRI or an ordinal RRI-derived utility without capturing the candidate view.
label: View Introspection Network

definition_long: VRS is part of the upstream Project Aria ecosystem, while ARIA-NBV primarily consumes ASE/ATEK-derived tensorized snippets for current experiments.
definition_short: File format used by Project Aria tooling to store multi-modal sensor recordings efficiently.
label: Virtual Reality Standard

definition_long: Project Aria pose and mapping products build on visual-inertial estimation, and ARIA-NBV depends on those pose streams for snippet state and candidate frame contracts.
definition_short: Pose-estimation method that combines visual measurements from cameras with inertial measurements from IMUs.
label: Visual-Inertial Odometry
